steps.title: Recognize the transformation rules for data sets
steps.content:
When a constant is added to every data point in a set:
  • Mean changes by that constant: if you add 13 to each value, the mean increases by exactly 13
  • Range stays the same: if the original set spans from min to max, adding 13 gives a new range of (max + 13) − (min + 13) = max − min

steps.title: Find the range of set B
steps.content:
From the dot plot for set A, the capacity values range from 2 μF to 14 μF.
Range of set A = 14 − 2 = 12 μF
Since adding 13 to each capacitor in set A does not change the spread, the range of set B is also 12 μF. This eliminates choices B and D immediately.

steps.title: Calculate the mean of set A from the dot plot
steps.content:
Reading the frequencies from the dot plot:
  • 2 μF: 5 capacitors
  • 5 μF: 5 capacitors
  • 8 μF: 2 capacitors
  • 11 μF: 4 capacitors
  • 14 μF: 5 capacitors
$$\text{Sum} = 2(5) + 5(5) + 8(2) + 11(4) + 14(5) = 10 + 25 + 16 + 44 + 70 = 165$$
$$\text{Mean of A} = \frac{165}{21} \approx 7.86\text{ μF}$$

steps.title: Apply the transformation to find the mean of set B
steps.content: Since each capacitor in set B has a capacity 13 μF greater than the corresponding capacitor in set A:$$\text{Mean of B} = \text{Mean of A} + 13 \approx 7.86 + 13 = 20.86\text{ μF}$$Rounding to the nearest integer, the mean of set B is approximately 21 μF. Combined with the range of 12 μF, that matches choice C — C is correct.
